authentication: Support OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow only; do not add email/password or username/password login.
Use provider abstraction with provider keys google, github, microsoft, and apple.
Issue short-lived JWT access tokens and rotate refresh tokens on each refresh.
Store refresh tokens as hashes, not plaintext.
authorization: Use role-based access control with USER and ADMIN only.
Apply ADMIN checks to every /api/v1/admin/* endpoint.
Enforce ownership on user booking and locker access endpoints.
jwt: Include sub as user public id, role, iat, exp, iss, aud, and jti.
Keep access token lifetime short, for example 10 to 15 minutes.
Use asymmetric signing for production when multiple services verify tokens.
oauth: Use state and PKCE for browser OAuth flow.
Validate issuer, audience/client id, nonce when applicable, and verified email from provider profile.
apiHardening: Use CORS allowlist for the React SPA origin.
Apply rate limits to OAuth start/callback, refresh, and locker access endpoints.
Return consistent error shapes with an optional errors array.
Do not leak internal ObjectId, provider raw tokens, stack traces, or hardware secrets.
